doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_round_p (mpfr/src/round_p.c L67-L135). Substrate-class helper used in transcendental Ziv-loop tails: given a raw limb-array approximation `bp` (little-endian; bp[bn-1] has MSB set), the error bound exponent `err0`, and the target precision `prec`, returns true iff the approximation can be rounded toward zero at precision `prec` (i.e. the err0-bit error bound doesn't straddle the rounding boundary). The algorithm: compute the position of the rounding boundary (bit `prec` from the MSB) and the error band (bits between `prec` and `err0`), then check the error band contains neither all-zero nor all-one — the only cases where rounding could go either way. Returns false (= 0 in C) if err0 <= 0, err0 <= prec, or prec >= total-bits — i.e. trivially not enough info. The TS port takes a bigint array (the limbs); bn = bp.length. Output is a TS boolean (not int 0/1, per the predicate-port convention).
c_signature: int mpfr_round_p(mp_limb_t *bp, mp_size_t bn, mpfr_exp_t err0, mpfr_prec_t prec)
divergence_from_c: C takes (mp_limb_t *bp, mp_size_t bn, mpfr_exp_t err0, mpfr_prec_t prec) and returns int (0/non-zero). The C function is a __MPFR_DECLSPEC export from mpfr-impl.h (not from mpfr.h), so external callers must declare it manually — we do exactly that in the golden_driver.c. TS takes (bp: readonly bigint[], err0: bigint, prec: bigint) and returns boolean. The limb array on the TS side uses GMP's little-endian convention (bp[0] is least-significant), matching the C contract exactly. bn is derived from bp.length (no separate length argument; the array carries it). The C side asserts bp[bn-1] & MPFR_LIMB_HIGHBIT (the MSB of the top limb is set); TS port mirrors via an explicit guard but treats violations as input errors via MPFRError('EPREC') rather than abort.
refs: mpfr/src/round_p.c L67-L135 — the C reference body (the production version; round_p_2 is the WANT_ASSERT debug variant).
mpfr/src/round_p.c L62-L66 — comment explaining the function's contract.
src/internal/mpfr/round_raw.ts — sibling substrate; same little-endian limb idiom.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts: GMP mpn limbs are LITTLE-ENDIAN by limb index'.
